steps.title: Locate the included angle
steps.content: The given side equality is $JK=MN$, and the given angles at $J$ and $M$ are equal. Angle $J$ is between sides $JK$ and $JL$, and angle $M$ is between sides $MN$ and $MP$.

steps.title: Choose the matching side
steps.content: To use side-angle-side congruence, the second side must be the other side adjacent to the given angle. Choice A gives a non-included side and creates an ambiguous side-side-angle setup. Choice C compares two angles inside only one triangle, and Choice D leaves only one side and one angle, which cannot determine the triangles or fix the remaining side lengths. The sufficient added information is $JL=MP$. The answer is B.
